label: Combinatorics
shortDescription: The art of counting, arranging, and optimizing discrete structures.
modulesDescription: Read the combinatorics chapters in order, or jump directly to the topic you need.
ogDescription: Master combinatorics: basic counting, permutations and combinations, binomial coefficients, pigeonhole and inclusion-exclusion, generating functions, Catalan/Stirling/Bell numbers, graph coloring, Ramsey theory, extremal graphs, enumerative techniques, Young tableaux, matroids, flows, linear programming, probabilistic method, Lovász Local Lemma, and additive combinatorics — with ELI5 explanations, worked examples, and practice questions.
courseDescription: A complete combinatorics course covering the fundamental principles of counting (product/sum rules, permutations, combinations), advanced counting with generating functions and recurrence relations, special numbers (Catalan, Stirling, Bell), graph-theoretic combinatorics (coloring, Ramsey, Turán, Szemerédi), enumerative methods (bijective proofs, Young tableaux, hook-length), combinatorial optimization (matroids, matchings, network flows, linear programming), and advanced probabilistic and algebraic tools (probabilistic method, Lovász Local Lemma, combinatorial nullstellensatz, additive bases). Features intuitive ELI5 sections, detailed step-by-step worked examples, common mistakes, and targeted practice questions for each lesson.

topics.title: Combinatorics
topics.description: The branch of mathematics concerned with counting, arranging, and selecting discrete objects under various constraints.

topics.title: Permutation
topics.description: An ordered arrangement of objects, where the sequence matters; counted by n! or P(n,k).

topics.title: Combination
topics.description: An unordered selection of objects; counted by binomial coefficients C(n,k) or  inom{n}{k}.

topics.title: Binomial coefficient
topics.description: The number  inom{n}{k} counting ways to choose k items from n, central to counting and algebra.

topics.title: Pigeonhole principle
topics.description: If more pigeons than holes, at least one hole has more than one; a powerful existence tool.

topics.title: Inclusion–exclusion principle
topics.description: A counting technique for the size of unions of sets accounting for overlaps with alternating signs.

topics.title: Generating function
topics.description: A formal power series whose coefficients encode a sequence, used to solve counting problems elegantly.

topics.title: Exponential generating function
topics.description: A variant of generating functions using n! in denominators, ideal for labeled structures and permutations.

topics.title: Recurrence relation
topics.description: An equation defining a sequence in terms of previous terms; solved to count combinatorial objects.

topics.title: Catalan number
topics.description: A famous sequence C_n = \frac{1}{n+1}\binom{2n}{n} counting dyck words, binary trees, non-crossing partitions, and more.

topics.title: Stirling number
topics.description: Numbers counting partitions of sets into non-empty subsets (second kind) or permutations into cycles (first kind).

topics.title: Bell number
topics.description: The number of partitions of a set; the sum of Stirling numbers of the second kind.

topics.title: Graph coloring
topics.description: Assigning colors to vertices so adjacent have different colors; chromatic number and applications to scheduling.

topics.title: Ramsey theory
topics.description: In any sufficiently large structure, order must appear; guarantees monochromatic cliques in edge colorings.

topics.title: Extremal graph theory
topics.description: The study of the maximum number of edges in graphs without certain subgraphs, e.g. Turán's theorem.

topics.title: Turán's theorem
topics.description: The maximum edges in a graph without a complete subgraph K_r; the extremal number ex(n, K_r).

topics.title: Szemerédi's theorem
topics.description: Any subset of integers with positive upper density contains arbitrarily long arithmetic progressions.

topics.title: Enumerative combinatorics
topics.description: The area focused on counting the number of elements in finite sets with combinatorial structure.

topics.title: Bijective proof
topics.description: A proof of equality of two counts by exhibiting a one-to-one correspondence between the sets.

topics.title: Young tableau
topics.description: A filling of a Young diagram with numbers increasing in rows and columns; used in symmetric group reps.

topics.title: Hook-length formula
topics.description: A formula giving the number of standard Young tableaux of a given shape via product of hook lengths.

topics.title: Robinson–Schensted correspondence
topics.description: A bijection between permutations and pairs of standard Young tableaux revealing the length of longest increasing subsequence.

topics.title: Matroid
topics.description: A structure generalizing linear independence in vector spaces and acyclic sets in graphs for optimization.

topics.title: Matching (graph theory)
topics.description: A set of edges without common vertices; maximum matchings, Hall's theorem, and applications to assignment.

topics.title: Network flow
topics.description: Modeling capacities on directed graphs with sources and sinks; the max-flow min-cut theorem.

topics.title: Linear programming
topics.description: Optimizing a linear objective function subject to linear inequality constraints; duality and simplex algorithm.

topics.title: Probabilistic method
topics.description: Proving existence of combinatorial objects by showing that a random construction succeeds with positive probability.

topics.title: Lovász Local Lemma
topics.description: A probabilistic tool showing that if bad events are locally dependent and have small probability, an outcome avoiding all exists.

topics.title: Combinatorial nullstellensatz
topics.description: An algebraic technique using the non-vanishing of polynomials to prove existence of points with prescribed properties.

topics.title: Additive combinatorics
topics.description: The study of sumsets, arithmetic progressions, bases, and additive structure within sets of integers.
